question.english: Technology is everywhere, but sometimes it fails us. Tell me about a time when you experienced a technology issue, such as an internet outage or a broken phone. What happened, and how did you handle it?
question.korean: 기술은 어디에나 있지만 때로는 우리를 실망시키기도 합니다. 인터넷 중단이나 스마트폰 고장 등 기술적인 문제를 겪었던 경험에 대해 이야기해 주세요. 어떤 일이 있었고 어떻게 대처하셨나요?
question.combo_context: 기기/기술 관련 콤보 질문 중 '과거에 겪은 기기 오작동 및 문제 해결 경험'을 묻는 3단계 질문입니다. 자연스러운 구어체 필러와 디스코스 마커를 사용해 상황을 생생하게 묘사하는 것이 핵심입니다.

model_answer.script:
To be completely honest with you, I used to be someone who was constantly glued to the screen, you know? But last summer, I had this major wake-up call when my phone suddenly had a breakdown. It just went pitch black and wouldn't turn on at all.

At first, I immediately felt a sense of dread. I mean, we rely on our phones for literally everything nowadays—navigation, payments, and communication. Suddenly, I felt completely cut off from the world. It was like, "Oh my god, what am I supposed to do now?"

But, looking back, the thing is, it turned out to be an eye-opening experience. Since I couldn't use my phone, I had to basically go cold turkey on social media for three whole days until it got repaired.

At first, it was incredibly frustrating. But as I came to terms with my situation, I started noticing things around me. I actually read a physical book, talked to my family face-to-face, and enjoyed the quietness. It really hit me that I didn't need to be online 24/7. It was the perfect accidental break from screens.

Now, I try to consciously unplug. It really changed how I view my relationship with technology.
model_answer.structure_note: 서론: To be completely honest with you~ (폰이 고장 났던 경험 소개) -> MP: At first, I immediately felt~ (당시 느꼈던 당혹감과 세상과의 단절감) -> 디테일 확장: But, looking back~ (폰 없이 3일간 강제로 소셜 미디어를 끊어야 했던 상황) -> 감정/생각: At first, it was~ (불안함에서 점차 여유를 찾고 깨달음을 얻은 과정) -> 마무리: Now, I try to~ (현재까지 이어지는 행동의 변화 및 교훈)
al_points: 구어체 필러인 'you know?', 'I mean', 'the thing is' 등을 문맥에 맞게 적재적소에 배치하여 자연스럽게 말을 이어가는 원어민스러운 흐름을 보여줍니다.
과거 시제(had, felt, couldn't, started)를 흔들림 없이 유지하면서 과거 행동과 깨달음을 효과적으로 서사했습니다.
감정을 나타내는 생생한 구어적 표현('felt a sense of dread', 'go cold turkey')을 사용해 상황의 극적인 면을 강조해 가산점을 유도합니다.

vocab.meaning: 화면에서 눈을 떼지 못하다, 화면을 계속 들여다보다
vocab.example: To be completely honest with you, I used to be someone who was constantly glued to the screen, you know?
vocab.collocation: be glued to the screen all day

vocab.example: But last summer, I had this major wake-up call when my phone suddenly had a breakdown.
At first, I immediately felt a sense of dread.
vocab.collocation: feel a deep sense of dread

vocab.example: Suddenly, I felt completely cut off from the world.
vocab.collocation: feel completely cut off from the world

vocab.meaning: 눈이 번쩍 뜨이는 경험, 새로운 깨달음을 주는 경험
vocab.example: But, looking back, the thing is, it turned out to be an eye-opening experience.
I had to basically go cold turkey on social media for three whole days until it got repaired.
But as I came to terms with my situation, I started noticing things around me.
My laptop ran out of juice right in the middle of the online interview.
I highly recommend trying a digital detox over the weekend to clear your head.

vocab.meaning: 기술을 잘 아는, 기기를 잘 다루는
vocab.example: Even though I'm quite tech-savvy, I had no idea how to fix my broken tablet.
Instead of working, he would just stare blankly at the computer screen.
The GPS went haywire and led us deep into the mountains.
I had butterflies in my stomach right before my presentation started.
vocab.collocation: always have butterflies in my stomach

vocab.example: It's healthy to unplug from technology at least once a week.
vocab.collocation: unplug from technology for a day

vocab.example: My grandfather is a total technophobe and refuses to use a smartphone.

vocab_quiz.question: 다음 중 '(나쁜 습관 등을) 단번에 완전히 끊다'라는 뜻을 가진 표현은 무엇인가요?
다음 중 '기계치, 기술 공포증이 있는 사람'을 뜻하는 단어는 무엇인가요?
다음 중 '기기가 통제 불능이 되거나 오작동하다'를 의미하는 표현은 무엇인가요?
빈칸에 알맞은 표현을 고르세요: "To be completely honest with you, I used to be someone who was constantly ________, looking at social media all day."
빈칸에 알맞은 표현을 고르세요: "When the power went out during the storm, I immediately felt a ________ because I couldn't charge my phone."
다음 문장의 'had a breakdown'을 자연스럽게 대체할 수 있는 표현은 무엇인가요? "My phone suddenly had a breakdown."
다음 문장의 'digital detox'와 가장 유사한 의미를 가진 표현은 무엇인가요? "I really needed a digital detox last week."

patterns.pattern: To be completely honest with you, [clause]
patterns.usage_today: To be completely honest with you, I used to be someone who was constantly glued to the screen, you know?
patterns.extra_example: To be completely honest with you, I didn't like the food at that restaurant at first.

patterns.usage_today: It really hit me that I didn't need to be online 24/7.
patterns.extra_example: It really hit me that life is too short to worry about small things.

review_words.new_example: When I am stressed by technology, I tend to gravitate towards spending time in nature.
Instead of staring at my phone on a Sunday afternoon, I decided to hit the gym.
Unplugging from social media is actually the best way for me to recharge my batteries.
I felt stuck in a rut when I realized I was spending four hours a day looking at short-form videos.

review_patterns.pattern: Instead of just -ing, I really want to...
review_patterns.new_example: Instead of just complaining about my broken phone, I really want to find a creative way to spend my offline time.
review_patterns.mini_mission: 이 패턴과 오늘 배운 단어 'digital detox'를 함께 사용해 문장을 말해 보세요. (예시 답변: "Instead of just lying on the bed all Sunday, I really want to try a digital detox to clear my mind.")

checklist: 오늘 배운 핵심 단어(glued to the screen, had a breakdown 등)를 최소 4개 이상 실제 스크립트 답변에 녹여 말했는가?
과거 고장 난 상황을 설명할 때 과거 시제(felt, was, couldn't 등)를 정확하게 유지했는가?
메인 포인트(MP)를 말한 뒤, 어떤 일들이 벌어졌는지 세부 디테일을 2개 이상 상세히 묘사했는가?
갑작스러운 기기 고장 당시에 느꼈던 감정이나 생각을 실감 나게 말했는가?
결론부에서 교훈이나 현재 변화된 습관을 언급하며 답변을 완성도 있게 마무리했는가?
의도치 않은 긴 침묵 없이 디스코스 마커나 필러(you know, I mean, the thing is)를 사용해 매끄럽게 발화했는가?
